name: Margo Moceyunas
school: Department of Astronomy and Planetary Science
focus: Glacial mineralogy • Permafrost deformation • Mars analog geomorphology
bio: Margo Moceyunas is a graduate student in Astronomy and Planetary Science at Northern Arizona University, where she also serves as a Graduate Teaching Assistant. Working under Dr. Alicia Marie Rutledge, her research focuses on understanding large-scale cryospheric processes like glacier retreat and permafrost deformation as well as the small-scale mineral alteration that occurs during these processes. Her research combines field work, remote sensing, and data analysis to study cold geomorphological processes and further understand both the Earth and Mars
